ORDER ON DEFENDANTS RAFAEL VAKNIN, SARA VAKNIN AND J & R AUTO SHOP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS RAFAEL VAKNIN, SARA VAKNIN AND J & R AUTO SHOP INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PAGES 29 AND 30 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT INDICATE THAT AT LEAST A PORTION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE TIME BARRED. THOSE LETTERS INDICATE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS AWARE OF DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED OVERCHARGING BY OCTOBER OF 2011. IT IS UNCLEAR WHEN THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ON THE BOOSTER CLAIM BEGAN TO RUN. PG. 39 OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS VAGUE REGARDING PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY OF THAT THEORY OF LIABILITY. PLAINTIFF, HOWEVER, IS REQUIRED TO PLEAD HIS DISCOVERY WITH PARTICULARITY. "IN ORDER TO RELY ON THE DISCOVERY RULE FOR DELAYED ACCRUAL OF A CAUSE OF ACTION, '[A] PLAINTIFF WHOSE COMPLAINT SHOWS ON ITS FACE THAT HIS CLAIM WOULD BE BARRED WITHOUT THE BENEFIT OF THE DISCOVERY RULE MUST SPECIFICALLY PLEAD FACTS TO SHOW (1) THE TIME AND MANNER OF DISCOVERY AND (2) THE INABILITY TO HAVE MADE EARLIER DISCOVERY DESPITE REASONABLE DILIGENCE.'" FOX V. ETHICON ENDO-SURGERY, INC. (2005) 35 CAL.4TH 797, 808 IN ADDITION, THE COURT INSTRUCTS PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD ULTIMATE FACTS TO SUPPORT HIS CLAIMS. MOST OF PLAINTIFF'S ATTACHMENTS ARE EVIDENTIARY FACTS THAT ARE NOT A PROPER PART OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489; DEPT. 302. =302/EHG

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-02-2016 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2016 Continued To Sep-06-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2015 Set For Trial On Jul-11-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-18-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Vaknin of Law Office of onahtan Vaknin telephone number 510-480-1430 for Defendant.
No other appearance was made in the matter.

Ruling: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS RAFAEL VAKNIN, SARA VAKNIN and J & R AUTO SHOP INC'S Motion For Sanctions is denied. Defendant provides no supporting evidence with the motion and does not demonstrate that the amended complaint was frivolous. In addition, the court has already given plaintiff leave to file a Second Amended Complaint that may address some of the cited pleading defects. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not Reported. (302/EHG)

Noice Of Motion And Motion To Strike 2nd Filing Of First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Nov-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Vaknin of Law Office of onahtan Vaknin telephone number 510-480-1430 for Defendant.
No other appearance was made in the matter.

Ruling: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS RAFAEL VAKNIN, SARA VAKNIN and J & R AUTO SHOP INC'S Motion To Strike 2nd Filing Of First Amended Complaint filed on 7/2/15 is granted. The filing of that pleading was unnecessary because plaintiff already filed the amended complaint on 6/19/15. The court was unaware that plaintiff filed an amended complaint when it sustained the demurrer to the original complaint on 6/24/15. The demurrer should have been taken off calendar. At any rate, all of this is largely moot. On 11/4/15, the court heard defendant's demurrer to the amended complaint and sustained that demurrer with 20 days leave to amend. Therefore, plaintiff has leave to file a Second Amended Complaint. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Vaknin of Law Office of onahtan Vaknin telephone number 510-480-1430 for Defendant.
No other appearance was made in the matter.

Ruling: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS RAFAEL VAKNIN, SARA VAKNIN and J & R AUTO SHOP INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is denied as moot. This motion is not supported by evidence or a separate statement of undisputed facts. The motion attacks the allegations of the amended complaint analogous to a demurrer or motion for judgment on the pleadings. The court already gave plaintiff leave to amend to address the pleading defects and file a Second Amended Complaint, however. Thus, the motion is moot. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not Reported. (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-04-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JONATHAN N. VAKNIN, TEL. NO. 1-510-480-1430 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS RAFAEL VAKNIN, SARA VAKNIN and J & R AUTO SHOP INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Pages 29 and 30 of the amended complaint indicate that at least a portion of plaintiff's claims are time barred. Those letters indicate that plaintiff was aware of defendant's alleged overcharging by October of 2011. It is unclear when the statute of limitations on the booster claim began to run. Pg. 39 of the amended complaint is vague regarding plaintiff's discovery of that theory of liability. Plaintiff, however, is required to plead his discovery with particularity. "In order to rely on the discovery rule for delayed accrual of a cause of action, '[a] plaintiff whose complaint shows on its face that his claim would be barred without the benefit of the discovery rule must specifically plead facts to show (1) the time and manner of discovery and (2) the inability to have made earlier discovery despite reasonable diligence.'" Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808 In addition, the court instructs plaintiff to plead ultimate facts to support his claims. Most of plaintiff's attachments are evidentiary facts that are not a proper part of the amended complaint. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
